LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO PLEAD COMPLIANCE WITH THE GOVERNMENT CLAIMS ACT. PLAINTIFF'S "CLAIMS," AS PLEADED, FAIL TO COMPLY WITH GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 910. THE SERIES OF "CLAIMS" THAT PLAINTIFF PRESENTED OVER THE COURSE OF YEARS DO NOT CONSTITUTE SUBSTANTIAL COMPLIANCE. SCHAEFER DIXON ASSOCIATES VS. SANTA ANA WATERSHED PROJECT AUTHORITY (1996) 48 CAL.APP.4TH 524. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-13-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, RAFAEL RODRIGUEZ, OMAR CARVALLO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES. Appearances: Samuel B. Rudolph of the Law Office of Samuel B. Rudolph (510.886.4876) for plaintiff and responding party; Rafal Ofierski of the Office of the City Attorney (554.4244) for defendants and moving parties.

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. The Court has yet to determine the 1st and 2nd causes of action. The court did not execute an order regarding the motion for judgment on the pleadings and the time to amend has not yet run. Motion for summary adjudication granted as to the F.E.H.A. retaliation, harassment, and discrimination causes of action. First, nearly all of plaintiff's claims are time-barred and the continuing violation doctrine does not apply. Second, plaintiff's complaint regarding the civil service commission's conduct lacks merit. Plaintiff fails to show pretext. Third, plaintiff fails to show harassing conduct or that he exhausted his administrative remedy. Fourth, individual defendants cannot be liable for retaliation. Further, the court overruled defendants' objections to exhibits attached to Mr. Rudolph's declaration. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. The Court signed an order on the motion for judgment on the pleadings that was heard on April 16, 2010. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgemnt On The Pleadings;
Off Calendar Apr-16-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings. Appearances: Samuel B. Rudolph, Esq. Attorney at Law, (510) 886-4876 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Rafal Ofierski, Deputy City Attorney, CCSF, (415) 554-4244 appearing for Defendant and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion granted with leave to amend for plaintiff to plead compliance with the Government Claims Act. Plaintiff's "claims," as pleaded, fail to comply with Government Code section 910. The series of "claims" that plaintiff presented over the course of years do not constitute substantial compliance. Schaefer Dixon Associates vs. Santa Ana Watershed Project Authority (1996) 48 Cal.App.4th 524. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Carlito Mandia, CSR # 9689
